messages.content:
You are a COBOL-to-English semantic synthesizer. Given an ordered list of Pass-2 propositions belonging to a single paragraph, produce a paragraph-level summary in the structured JSON format below. Respond with ONLY the JSON object — no prose, no code fence, no commentary.

Required response JSON schema:
{
  "paragraph": "<copy from request>",
  "group_label": "<short human-readable label, 2-5 words>",
  "semantic_pattern": "<one of: guard-with-override, accumulation, state-machine, delegation, sequential, conditional-branch, cics-interaction, file-io, unknown>",
  "summary": "<one-sentence paragraph-level description>",
  "member_seqs": [<int>, ...],
  "business_rules": [
    {"id": "BR-NNN", "rule": "<English statement>", "rule_type": "<guard|transform|lookup|audit|display>", "confidence": "<high|medium|low>"}
  ]
}

Rule 10: `semantic_pattern` MUST be one of the nine enum values. `unknown` is permitted ONLY when the paragraph is genuinely unclassifiable; it triggers a human-review gate and blocks merge. Do NOT use `unknown` as a default — prefer `sequential` for linear flows and `conditional-branch` for IF/EVALUATE gating.
Rule 9: all data names in business_rules[].rule text must be fully qualified if the underlying Pass-2 proposition carries a qualified name.

Program ID: CBTRN01C
Paragraph: MAIN-PARA
Proposition count: 30

Ordered propositions (seq → verb → proposition):
  seq=1  DISPLAY  →  Display output message
  seq=2  PERFORM  →  Execute the 0000-DALYTRAN-OPEN procedure
  seq=3  PERFORM  →  Execute the 0100-CUSTFILE-OPEN procedure
  seq=4  PERFORM  →  Execute the 0200-XREFFILE-OPEN procedure
  seq=5  PERFORM  →  Execute the 0300-CARDFILE-OPEN procedure
  seq=6  PERFORM  →  Execute the 0400-ACCTFILE-OPEN procedure
  seq=7  PERFORM  →  Execute the 0500-TRANFILE-OPEN procedure
  seq=8  PERFORM  →  Execute a procedure
  seq=9  IF [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  When END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N'
  seq=10  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 1000-DALYTRAN-GET-NEXT procedure
  seq=11  IF [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  When END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N'
  seq=12  DISPLAY [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Display output message
  seq=13  MOVE [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N'] [modifies=WS-XREF-READ-STATUS]  →  Set WS-XREF-READ-STATUS to 0
  seq=14  MOVE [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Move data between 
  seq=15  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 2000-LOOKUP-XREF procedure
  seq=16  IF [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  When END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N'
  seq=17  MOVE [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N'] [modifies=WS-ACCT-READ-STATUS]  →  Set WS-ACCT-READ-STATUS to 0
  seq=18  MOVE [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Move data between 
  seq=19  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 3000-READ-ACCOUNT procedure
  seq=20  IF [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  When END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N'
  seq=21  DISPLAY [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Display output message
  seq=22  DISPLAY [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Display output message
  seq=23  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 9000-DALYTRAN-CLOSE procedure
  seq=24  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 9100-CUSTFILE-CLOSE procedure
  seq=25  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 9200-XREFFILE-CLOSE procedure
  seq=26  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 9300-CARDFILE-CLOSE procedure
  seq=27  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 9400-ACCTFILE-CLOSE procedure
  seq=28  PERFORM [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Execute the 9500-TRANFILE-CLOSE procedure
  seq=29  DISPLAY [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Display output message
  seq=30  GOBACK [IF END-OF-DAILY-TRANS-FILE = 'N']  →  Return control to caller

Produce the paragraph-level semantic synthesis per the schema in the system prompt. Include every proposition's `seq` in `member_seqs`. Derive business rules ONLY from propositions that carry a real guard / transform / lookup / audit / display intent — do not invent rules for routine MOVE/SET/PERFORM statements.
